Issue Nº 01 · The Road Trip Edition
Late August · France & Italy
Featuring
Itay · Roni · Ari · Libi
A four-person expedition, three possible routes

Burgundy → Rome

From our friends' table in Burgundy to our friends' door near Rome. One car, four of us, and a whole summer coastline to argue over. Pick a route below.

01Chasing the BlueFor the sea-lovers 02The Long TableFor the food-lovers 03The Wonder HuntA bit of everything
The Naglers · Aug '26
Itay
Roni
Ari
Libi
The premise

Same start. Same finish. Three completely different trips in between.

01 / BLUEChasing the Blue
Hug the coast the whole way. Riviera sand, two slow nights in the Cinque Terre, a hidden cove in the Tuscan wild.
02 / TABLEThe Long Table
Turn inland over the Alps into Piedmont's wine and truffle country, then the great hill towns of Tuscany.
03 / WONDERThe Wonder Hunt
One jaw-dropper a day — a cliff village, a tower with trees, a farm pool, swimmable hot-spring waterfalls.

The Crew

Four travellers · one car · zero agreement on lunch
01The water-chaser

Chasing
the Blue

If you can see the sea, you're on the right road.

We string the whole trip along the coastline. Every morning opens with a swim, every afternoon ends with feet in the sand, and the drives are short hops from one stretch of turquoise to the next — Antibes, two unhurried nights in the Cinque Terre, then a secret Tuscan cove before Rome.

N 44.13° · E 9.68° Vernazza, Cinque Terre
4 days
On the road
~1,310 km
Distance
5 swims
Sea days
2 nights
In Cinque Terre
Day 01 · Fri
Burgundy → Antibes
Long first push, ending on soft Riviera sand for a sunset swim.
Sleep · Antibes
Day 02 · Sat
→ Cinque Terre
Cross into Italy; park in Levanto, train to Vernazza for the evening.
Sleep · Levanto 1/2
Day 03 · Sun
Cinque Terre, slow
A whole day of villages, a boat ride and swims. No long driving.
Sleep · Levanto 2/2
Day 04 · Mon
Cala Violina → Rome
Forest walk to a wild cove, a last Tuscan swim, then south to friends.
Arrive · Rome
Best if the kids would trade any castle for one more hour in the water.
02The slow gourmet

The Long
Table

We eat our way across the map.

This route climbs inland over the Alps into Piedmont — Italy's quiet capital of wine, hazelnut and truffle — then drops into the storybook hill towns of Tuscany. Market mornings, long lunches under the vines, gelato in a town of stone towers, pecorino tasted where it's made. Less coast, far more flavour.

N 44.61° · E 7.94° Barolo, Piedmont
4 days
On the road
~1,260 km
Distance
3 towns
Hill towns
1 night
On a Tuscan farm
Day 01 · Fri
Burgundy → Alba
Over the Alps into Piedmont. Hazelnuts, vines, a first great dinner.
Sleep · Langhe hills
Day 02 · Sat
Barolo → Lucca
Vineyard morning, then south to the walled city you bike around on top.
Sleep · Lucca
Day 03 · Sun
Siena → Val d'Orcia
The great shell square, San Gimignano's towers, sunset over the hills.
Sleep · Val d'Orcia
Day 04 · Mon
Pienza → Rome
Cheese tasting and a last hill-town stroll before the run to friends.
Arrive · Rome
Best if the best part of any holiday is sitting at the table a little too long.
03The balanced adventure

The Wonder
Hunt

One unforgettable thing every single day.

The everything route. A different kind of wonder each day: a cliff village hung over the sea, a medieval tower with oak trees growing on its roof, a working farm with a pool, and the finale — warm thermal waterfalls in the Tuscan wild you can actually swim in. Beach, hill town and magic, in the right doses for all four of us.

N 42.65° · E 11.51° Cascate del Mulino, Saturnia
4 days
On the road
~1,290 km
Distance
4 wows
Big moments
Springs
The grand finale
Day 01 · Fri
Burgundy → Antibes
Provence lunch, then a calm shallow beach for the first swim.
Sleep · Antibes
Day 02 · Sat
Èze → Cinque Terre
A cliff-top village, then the postcard coast at sunset.
Sleep · Levanto
Day 03 · Sun
Lucca → Val d'Orcia
The tower with trees, gelato in San Gimignano, a pool at the farm.
Sleep · Val d'Orcia
Day 04 · Mon
Saturnia → Rome
Swim in warm waterfalls, a long grill lunch, then south to friends.
Arrive · Rome
Best if you can't choose, and you'd rather have a little of everything.
Over to the family

So — which one do we live?

Same beginning, same happy ending with friends. Three very different sets of memories in between. Have a look, then everyone calls their pick and I'll lock the hotels and the dinner tables.
